item.title: EDP Bets Big on Australia in $1 Billion Renewables Push in Asia
item.description: EDP SA’s renewables unit will invest about $1 billion in green energy projects across Asia-Pacific through 2028, with a focus on developing its presence in Australia.

item.title: Dutch Climate Minister Says Talks to Quit Fossil Fuels Have Momentum
item.description: Countries that move away from oil and gas will be “shielding their economies against the kind of price shocks that we’re seeing currently,” said Stientje van Veldhoven.

item.title: Atome Sees War Driving Shifts That Boost Low-Carbon Fertilizer
item.description: The low-carbon fertilizer industry is getting a boost as buyers shift to long-term contracts amid rising geopolitical risks, according to the top executive at Atome PLC.

item.title: How Global Deforestation Declined 36% in 2025
item.description: The world still lost an area of rainforest roughly the size of Denmark

item.title: France Unveils Fossil-Fuel Exit Plan at Colombia Climate Summit
item.description: The country aims to exit coal by 2030, oil by 2045 and natural gas by 2050.

item.title: Blackstone to Invest €2 Billion in Renewables Developer Eurowind
item.description: Blackstone Inc. has agreed to invest as much as €2 billion ($2.3 billion) in Eurowind Energy, a pan-European renewables developer and independent power producer.

item.title: Global Forest Loss Fell Sharply in 2025 After Hitting Record High
item.description: Clearing of the world’s tropical forests, whether for farmland or by wildfires, declined by 36% last year, according to the Global Forest Watch initiative.

item.title: Mirova Green Fund Exits Philippine Debt After Corruption Scandal
item.description: Mirova SA’s flagship green bond fund exited its position in Philippine debt following a corruption scandal that raised concerns investors may have inadvertently financed flood-control projects now under investigation for graft.

item.title: Chinese Wind Firm Ming Yang Doubles Down on Europe After UK Snub
item.description: Ming Yang Smart Energy Group Ltd., a major Chinese maker of offshore wind turbines, aims to finalize a European site for a factory by June, after the UK rejected its plan to build a plant in Scotland.

item.title: Unreported Coal Mine Methane Imperils Climate Action, Ember Says
item.description: Nations that fail to report coal mine methane emissions could be missing out on cheap opportunities to slash releases of the potent greenhouse gas, according to a report from energy think tank Ember.

item.title: Red-State AGs Call Out Credit-Rating Companies Over ESG
item.description: A group of state attorneys general wrote to the US Securities and Exchange Commission and major credit-rating firms, raising concerns about the use of environmental, social and governance factors in downgrade decisions.

item.title: China Solar Giant Longi Says First-Quarter Loss Widened
item.description: Chinese solar giant Longi Green Energy Technology Co. saw its net loss widened in the first quarter, despite stronger module pricing and exports offset overcapacity in the sector.

item.title: Hungarian Nuclear Plant to Cut Block 2 Capacity 50% for Repairs
item.description: Hungary’s nuclear energy authority said output at one block of the country’s sole atomic power station will be cut by 50% from April 29 for repairs on a generator support system.

item.title: From the UK to South Korea, Consumers Turn to EVs as Oil Prices Spike
item.description: March data reveals surge in EV sales from the UK to South Korea

item.title: Spiking Oil Prices Spurred a EV Buying Spree in March
item.description: Electric car sales surged in Europe and parts of Asia, counteracting a slowdown in the US and China.

item.title: Commonwealth Files to Connect First Fusion Plant to US Grid
item.description: Commonwealth Fusion Systems, a company backed by Bill Gates and venture capitalist John Doerr, is seeking permission to connect what may be the world’s first commercial fusion power plant to the biggest US electric grid.

item.title: China Weighing a Second Green Sovereign Bond Sale in London
item.description: China is considering another green sovereign bond sale in London this year as the nation looks to expand its presence in international debt markets and fund climate action.

item.title: Samsung SDI Posts Smaller-Than-Expected Loss; Sending Gain
item.description: South Korean battery maker Samsung SDI Co. reported a smaller-than-expected first-quarter loss, helping its shares extend a blistering rally, as demand for energy storage systems and electric cars in Europe helped cushion the impact of dwindling EV support in the US.

item.title: CATL Inks First Major Deal to Provide Sodium-Ion Battery Storage
item.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. said it signed a three-year deal to provide sodium-ion batteries to Beijing HyperStrong Technology Co., a domestic manufacturer of power equipment.

item.title: India’s Heat Wave Creates New Inflation Risks After Oil Surge
item.description: India is facing inflation threats from heat waves and below normal rainfall this year, creating new economic pressures for policymakers already grappling with soaring energy costs.

item.title: Southeast Asia Office Workers Swelter in Energy-Saving Drive, Heat Wave
item.description: A heat wave sweeping across Southeast Asia is making offices even warmer, as workers continue to adjust to energy-saving measures put in place by governments due to the war in Iran.

item.title: War Hastens an Indonesian Biofuels Push That Has Global Stakes
item.description: Four trucks and a passenger bus have just completed a 40,000-kilometer (25,000-mile) road trip around Java. The epic journey — a distance equivalent to a circumnavigation of the globe — will help to determine whether Indonesia can deliver one of the world’s most ambitious biofuel-blending mandates in the next few months.

item.title: Trump Cancels More Wind Leases to Spur Oil and Gas Investment
item.description: Two offshore wind developers will give up federal leases and instead commit funds to fossil fuel projects under agreements with the Trump administration, the Interior Department said Monday.

item.title: Clean Energy ETF Attracts Giant Inflows This Year: ESG Investing
item.description: Clean energy stocks have been generating enormous market gains, and no exchange-traded fund has benefited more than the First Trust Nasdaq Clean Edge Smart Grid Infrastructure Index Fund.

item.title: Climate Finance Funds Attract Record Inflows in ‘Feast or Famine’ Year
item.description: Investors are moving toward supporting deployment-ready technologies, especially in the energy sector.

item.title: Economists Want to Know How Climate Change Raises Prices
item.description: The impacts of a warming world are showing up in the cost of living

item.title: Little Rain, Big Risks Ahead for Wheat Crops Across US Plains
item.description: Without sufficient moisture from rainfall or irrigation, wheat shoots struggle to fill out and produce grain

item.title: Sungrow First-Quarter Profit Slides as Competition Intensifies
item.description: Sungrow Power Supply Co., a top Chinese manufacturer of new energy equipment, said its first-quarter net income declined 40% from a year earlier, amid intensifying competition in the energy storage sector as more players enter the market.

item.title: Colorado River Water Woes Threaten Arizona’s AI Boom
item.description: Data center water demand in the state is slated to soar as the Colorado River dries up.

item.title: Pakistan Risks More Power Blackouts on Severe Heat Wave Forecast
item.description: Pakistan issued a heat wave warning for parts of the country, which threatens to strain the power grid and risks further blackouts, as the nation grapples with energy shortages due to the conflict in the Middle East.
